$comment: Authoritative schema for plan.json consumed by forgectl.
Derived from forgectl/state/types.go and forgectl/state/validate.go.
PATH RESOLUTION:
All paths in 'refs[].path' and 'items[].ref' are resolved relative to
the directory containing plan.json (filepath.Dir(plan.json)).
Example: if plan.json is at api/.forge_workspace/implementation_plan/plan.json,
then ref path '../../specs/foo.md' resolves to api/specs/foo.md.
Paths MUST NOT contain '#anchor' fragments — forgectl runs os.Stat()
on the raw path string. 'notes/foo.md#section' will fail validation.
VALIDATION RULES:
- Top-level: only 'context', 'refs', 'layers', 'items' are allowed.
- Every item must appear in exactly one layer.
- Items may only depend on items in the same or earlier layer.
- No dependency cycles.
- All depends_on IDs must exist in the items array.
- All refs paths must exist on disk.
- All item ref paths must exist on disk.
- Test categories must be: 'functional', 'rejection', or 'edge_case'.
IMPLEMENTING PHASE:
When forgectl transitions from planning to implementing, it adds
'passes' (string, default 'pending') and 'rounds' (int, default 0)
to every item. Do not include these fields when drafting.

context.$comment: Domain and project metadata. Only 'domain' and 'module' are recognized by forgectl.
context.domain: <string, required> Domain name matching the top-level directory
context.module: <string, required> Go module name or package name (e.g. 'spectacular/api')

refs.$comment: Reference file entries. Array of objects, NOT strings. Optional (omitempty).
refs.path: <string> Relative path from plan.json directory. Must exist on disk. No #anchors.

layers.$comment: Ordered dependency tiers. Layer N items may only depend on layer 0..N items.
layers.name: <string, required> Human-readable layer name
items: <string[]> Item IDs belonging to this layer. Every item must appear in exactly one layer.

items.id: <string, required> Unique item identifier. Convention: package.concern (e.g. 'config.load')
items.name: <string, required> Short action-oriented name
items.description: <string, required> One or two sentences explaining what and why
depends_on: <string[], required> Item IDs that must complete first. Use empty array [] for no deps.
files: <string[], optional (omitempty)> File paths to create or modify, relative to domain root
items.spec: <string, optional (omitempty)> Spec filename reference. Single string only, not array. No #anchors.
items.ref: <string, optional (omitempty)> Notes file path relative to plan.json directory. Must exist on disk. No #anchors.

tests.$comment: Use empty array [] for items with no tests (e.g. dependency bootstrapping).
tests.description: <string, required> What this test verifies — specific enough to evaluate unambiguously

minimal_item.name: Service config type definitions
minimal_item.description: Go structs for validated service endpoint configuration.

tests.description: Three named fields, not a map
